title: Japanese Battered Snapper
description: A simple Japanese-inspired recipe for a delicious battered snapper.

ingredients.name: Red Snapper Fillets
ingredients.notes: Fresh fish for the best taste

instructions: 1. In a shallow dish, mix together the flour and a pinch of salt.
2. In a separate dish, crack an egg and whisk it lightly.
3. Dip each snapper fillet into the flour mixture, coating it evenly.
4. Then, dip the floured fillet into the egg mixture, making sure it's fully coated.
5. Finally, roll the fillet in the Panko knots to coat evenly.
6. Place the coated snapper fillet on a plate or tray.
7. Heat about 1/2-inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
8. When the oil is hot, add 2-3 coated snapper fillets to the skillet.
9. Cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
10. Repeat with the remaining snapper fillets.
11. Remove the snapper from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
12. Serve hot with your favorite sides, such as steamed vegetables or a side salad.
